description: Deterministic regex patterns for prompt injection detection. Applied BEFORE Stage 0 pre-filter on all external data. Maintained by Chief of Staff during self-improvement loop.

categories.name: role_injection
categories.description: Attempts to inject system/assistant/user role markers into data

categories.name: instruction_override
categories.description: Attempts to override or replace the agent's instructions

categories.name: prompt_leaking
categories.description: Attempts to extract the agent's system prompt or instructions

categories.name: encoded_payloads
categories.description: Suspicious encoded content that may hide injection attempts
categories.notes: Base64 strings >50 chars, Cyrillic homoglyphs, zero-width characters. Decode first, then check decoded content against other categories.

categories.name: code_injection
categories.description: HTML/JS injection attempts in data fields

categories.name: social_engineering
categories.description: Social engineering attempts — flagged but NOT stripped (may be legitimate business content)

strip_and_flag.description: Remove matched pattern (replace with [SANITIZED]), add injection_flagged metadata to record, log to JSONL audit log

flag_only.description: Add injection_flagged metadata but do NOT modify the content. May be legitimate business language.

decode_check_strip.description: Decode the content (Base64, Unicode normalization), check decoded content against other categories, strip if malicious

escalation.1_flag: Proceed with stripped content. Tier 2 sees the flag during review.
escalation.2_flags: Proceed with extra Tier 2 scrutiny note.
escalation.3_plus_flags: Auto-reject. Do NOT send to Stage 0. Log as injection_blocked. Post to priority board as urgent_review.
escalation.source_blocked: If same source triggers 5+ flags in 24 hours, add to blocklist. Alert via Telegram.
